# Implementation Plan: Dashboard Comprehensive E2E and UI Validation

**Branch**: `005-dashboard-e2e-validation` | **Date**: 2026-05-14 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/005-dashboard-e2e-validation/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by `/speckit-plan`. It defines the validation design for a complete dashboard E2E/UI pass. The deliverable is a reproducible QA validation package and evidence contract, not a dashboard runtime change.

## Summary

Create a comprehensive dashboard E2E and UI validation package that proves every dashboard page, element, action, visual state, source-data boundary, and privacy constraint with browser-visible evidence. The validation uses the existing local dashboard command as the target, launches it with isolated temporary state, drives a real browser for UI behavior and screenshots, supplements observations with local HTTP and CLI checks, and records pass/fail/blocked evidence in structured QA artifacts.

The plan explicitly validates high-risk behavior called out by the user: Configuration field mapping controls must be selectable field controls populated from discovered source fields, field sync must update every mapping honestly, and clicking or focusing fields must not unexpectedly scroll the page to the top or trigger page navigation.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.9.x on Node.js `>=22`, matching the existing CLI and dashboard implementation.  
**Primary Dependencies**: Existing project dependencies only for the dashboard target. Browser validation is performed with an external real-browser automation capability and existing command-line tools; no new runtime package is required by this planning feature.  
**Storage**: Existing dashboard-local files under isolated temporary config/auth/audit/research paths for validation runs, browser localStorage for language/session UI state, and project-local QA evidence files under `specs/005-dashboard-e2e-validation/qa/`. No dashboard database is introduced.  
**Testing**: Existing `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, and `pnpm format:check` verify the repository baseline. Dashboard validation uses real browser screenshots, accessibility snapshots, console logs, network request summaries, scroll/focus state observations, and local HTTP/CLI probes.  
**Target Platform**: Developer workstation or agent environment capable of running the local dashboard command and opening a Chromium-compatible browser.  
**Project Type**: Single TypeScript CLI package with a local web dashboard plus QA validation artifacts.  
**Performance Goals**: Dashboard validation should collect initial evidence for all seven pages in one run, with each page action producing observable evidence within 10 seconds unless a live Lark dependency is blocked or unavailable.  
**Constraints**: Validation must not mutate real user config/auth/audit/research files. Live Lark checks must use a test account and test Bitable or be marked blocked. Validation must not report inferred behavior as verified without browser-visible or command-output evidence.  
**Scale/Scope**: Seven dashboard pages, global shell controls, all visible interactions, desktop and mobile visual states, empty/partial/error states, language switching, secret redaction, and live-Lark-dependent flows when credentials are available.

## Constitution Check

_GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design._

- **AI consumer contract**: PASS. The validation package defines machine-readable scenario IDs, expected results, evidence artifacts, pass/fail/blocked statuses, defect findings, and QA reports so an AI agent can consume results without guessing.
- **Evidence sources**: PASS. Planning evidence comes from the user requests in this conversation, [spec.md](./spec.md), the dashboard design reference at `specs/004-add-dashboard-command/design.md`, the dashboard HTTP contract at `specs/004-add-dashboard-command/contracts/dashboard-http-contract.md`, current repository scripts in `package.json`, current dashboard source files under `src/dashboard/`, and constitution rules in `.specify/memory/constitution.md`.
- **Unsupported-claim prevention**: PASS. Live Lark success is not claimed without a test account and test Bitable. Browser behavior is not claimed without screenshots, snapshots, console/network evidence, or explicit blocked reasons. Runtime observations override assumptions when they conflict.
- **Reproducible validation**: PASS. The plan defines isolated dashboard startup, deterministic local paths, repository baseline commands, browser evidence requirements, and explicit blocked handling for unavailable external credentials.
- **Report traceability**: PASS. Final reports separate observed facts, assumptions, blocked dependencies, failed expectations, defect reproduction steps, evidence artifacts, risks, and developer follow-up.

Post-design re-check: PASS. [research.md](./research.md), [data-model.md](./data-model.md), [contracts/dashboard-e2e-coverage-contract.md](./contracts/dashboard-e2e-coverage-contract.md), [contracts/browser-evidence-contract.md](./contracts/browser-evidence-contract.md), [contracts/qa-report-contract.md](./contracts/qa-report-contract.md), and [quickstart.md](./quickstart.md) preserve evidence-backed outputs, blocked-state handling, no unsupported claims, and traceable QA reporting.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/005-dashboard-e2e-validation/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   ├── browser-evidence-contract.md
│   ├── dashboard-e2e-coverage-contract.md
│   └── qa-report-contract.md
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md
└── tasks.md
```

`tasks.md` is intentionally not created by `/speckit-plan`; it is the expected output of `/speckit-tasks`.

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
AGENTS.md
package.json
specs/
└── 005-dashboard-e2e-validation/
    ├── plan.md
    ├── research.md
    ├── data-model.md
    ├── quickstart.md
    └── contracts/

specs/005-dashboard-e2e-validation/
└── qa/
    ├── CASE.md
    ├── RUN-001.md
    ├── REPORT-001.md
    └── artifacts/
```

**Structure Decision**: Keep the validation design and actual validation evidence under the active Spec Kit feature directory because this repository is an independent project and should not write QA artifacts into `../hybrid-im-knowledge/04_QA_tasks/`. Do not add a new frontend package, browser test framework, or dashboard runtime module in this planning phase. If a later task automates part of the run, it should still emit the same evidence and report contracts defined here.

## Complexity Tracking

No Constitution Check violations require justification. The plan adds validation artifacts and evidence contracts around the existing dashboard surface rather than introducing a new product subsystem.
